phases.phase: A0 (PREREQ, no MC) - Pre-registration + infra fixes that unblock everything
phases.goal: Land the small set of code/registration fixes the two reviews proved are mandatory BEFORE any headline MC runs, so the downstream cells are well-defined and statistically valid. This is the highest-value, near-zero-compute work; it must run first.
phases.run_matrix: (1) PRE-REGISTER, hash-bound in PROGRESS adopted-defaults BEFORE any A2-A5 run, two rules: (a) FEASIBILITY-GATE rule for the stressed headline regime - the performance comparison J is UN-GATED (J = 1 - macro_P_correct, NO feasible_point gate) because the verified dynamic-MC F_wrong is ~0.033-0.0525 for EVERY policy (distance 0.0525, uniform 0.035, trained 0.0525) while the urban_default profile keeps eps_w=eps_s=1e-3, so the gated headline_cost returns +inf for ALL policies -> scale_regret/normalized_scale_regret/feasibility_retention all evaluate to NaN. Feasibility (F_wrong/F_split UCB vs eps) is reported SEPARATELY as its own reliability column, never folded into the regret denominator. F_split is identically 0 so eps_s is fine; only the F_wrong gate is removed from the performance comparison. (b) the R_d(N)=round(6*sqrt(N/120)) service rule is ALREADY pre-registered (ladder 6/10/14/19/30/54 verified) - re-confirm it is unchanged. (2) CODE: add min_seeds + headline params actually passed by every result writer: headline cells call validate_manifest(..., min_seeds=5, headline=True); reduced-seed cells call it with min_seeds=<actual> and compute_limited=True (validate_manifest defaults to min_seeds=1, so >=5 is NOT auto-enforced - it must be asserted). (3) CODE: add a seed-level nested-bootstrap estimator (resample model seeds, then trials within seed; paired CRN across policies at shared scene seeds) as the headline CI for every separation/regret claim; the aggregate_seed_macros pooled-binomial Wilson CI (n_total = trials*seeds) is demoted to a DIAGNOSTIC only - it conflates model-seed init variance with within-MC Bernoulli draws and understates uncertainty. (4) CODE: add a snapshot_steps callback to train_esp_reinforce (it currently has no breakpoint save) + a unit test that resumed-vs-continuous trajectories match, so A2 intermediate budgets are real. (5) CODE: extend evaluate_macro to pool the fields DynamicMCResult ALREADY carries - basin_tau_correct_mean (=T_confirm), latency_cvar, mean_energy, energy_cvar - into the macro block (plumbing + 1 unit test). EXPLICITLY do NOT claim D95/D99/basin-conditioned CVaR99 as plumbing: they have no producer in the MC and are DROPPED from the A3 plot set (relabeled compute-limited-deferred, spec 9.1) rather than mislabeled zero-cost.
phases.constraints_addressed: Fixes the PLAN-FATAL feasibility-gate NaN (both reviews, violation #1) by pre-registering an un-gated J + separate reliability reporting, hash-bound and not tuned post-hoc. Enforces >=5-seed headline as an asserted gate not an assumption (#7). Replaces the wrong pooled-binomial separation interval with the spec-9.3 nested seed bootstrap + paired CRN (#9.3). Makes A2 intermediate snapshots real (not falsely free). Honestly scopes the evaluate_macro tail extension (T_confirm/energy plumbing yes; D99/CVaR99 new-code, dropped+labeled).
phases.notes: Zero MC. This phase exists because BOTH adversarial reviews independently flagged the feasibility-gate as plan-fatal and the seed-pooling as statistically invalid; neither can be deferred because every A2-A5 headline number depends on them. The pre-registered eps_w decision (un-gate the performance comparison) is the single most important fix - without it the entire A3 scale-regret headline is NaN. Must be committed (hash-bound) before any A2 eval run so it cannot be retro-tuned.

phases.phase: A2 - G-ESP-TRAINING-BUDGET (MC-faithful budget curve RISES vs EV1 flat)
phases.goal: Under the dynamic-MC judge (full physics), show held-out macro_P_correct RISES with training budget under per-node-credit MC-faithful REINFORCE, contrasted with the EV1 analytic FLAT curve (0.422 at every budget). Headline claim: MONOTONE-or-documented-plateau AND trained > uniform separated under the SEED-LEVEL test (not the pooled binomial). trained-vs-distance reported honestly (EV5 ~96% of distance; expected NOT separated even at 5 seeds - the +0.040 effect is narrower than the seed-level spread).
phases.run_matrix: Single headline regime mm_high(base_err=0.35, corr=0.25) R_d=6 N=120 grid(5,5,3) - identical to A1/EV4/EV5 so all points are comparable. Budget axis (training steps) = {0(init), 40, 80, 150}. Mechanism: re-run the 5-seed x 150-step REINFORCE trajectory ONCE using the new snapshot_steps callback (A0) to save state_dict at steps 40/80/150 along the SAME persistent-Adam trajectory; the init(step 0) point is the untrained GNN. This is a real ~73 min re-train (NOT free) - train_esp_reinforce had no breakpoint save and the live A1 job ran the unmodified trainer, so the in-flight A1 trajectory cannot be retro-snapshotted; the steps150 checkpoints from A1 are reused for the 150 point only if their trajectory matches, otherwise the snapshot re-train supersedes A1 for the budget axis. Then run_phase_a2_eval.py loads each (seed,budget) checkpoint and calls evaluate_macro at N=120, scene_seeds {0,1} x 200 trials = 400/seed, across all 5 seeds per budget, reported with BOTH the seed-level nested bootstrap CI (headline) AND across_seed_sd_P_correct. distance(0.4275) and uniform(0.3675) reference lines re-eval'd ONCE each at the same 400x2 budget under matched CRN for a paired overlay (+2 cells).
phases.model_seeds: 5 (seeds 0-4; full headline, NOT compute-limited). One ~73-min re-train-with-snapshots is the BASELINE A2 cost (reclassified from the original plan's 'free if hook lands' - verified the hook does not exist and the live trainer cannot be retro-snapshotted).
phases.trials_per_cell: 200 trials x 2 scene seeds = 400/seed per budget across 5 seeds. Headline CI = seed-level nested bootstrap over the 5 per-seed P_correct (each seed = one observation), paired-CRN against uniform/distance; the pooled-2000 Wilson is reported only as a diagnostic. scene_seeds=2 is EXPLICITLY labeled a compute-limited reduction vs the spec-9.1 >=30 minimum (this label was silently missing before and applies to EVERY cell in the campaign).
phases.constraints_addressed: Headline judge = dynamic-MC basin first-hitting full physics (evaluate_macro, link_override=None). >=5 seeds (full headline). macrostate_v2. train==eval distribution (corr_strength=0.25 threaded both sides). EV1 analytic 0.422 cited ONLY as the flat-baseline contrast, never as a headline number. Spec-4.5 plateau clause invoked explicitly if 150 does not exceed 80 within the seed-level spread. scene_seeds=2 labeled compute-limited (9.1).
phases.notes: EV5's 40/80 points (0.392/0.410) are RELABELED an independent prior-run reference (2 seeds, different trajectory/base_seed than A1's 100*(s+1)), NOT a cross-check of the A2 snapshots - presenting them as confirming EV5 would conflate two training runs. Monotonicity is verified empirically on the A2 trajectory; the A1 train curve oscillates (0.39,0.41,0.32,0.43...0.51) so RISING is a held-out-eval claim shown at the chosen budgets, not assumed. If trained-vs-uniform fails the seed-level test (the binding statistical risk - the +0.043 uniform gap is comparable to the -0.017 distance gap), A2 reports 'rises but not seed-level-separated' and the verdict defaults toward 13.2. Drops the spec-4.3 mixed-scale {100,300,1000} budget curve -> single N=120 scale (the RISE-vs-FLAT claim is regime-structural; the surrogate was flat HERE), labeled compute-limited.

phases.phase: A3a - SCALE-EXPERTS (regret denominator) + evaluate_macro tail plumbing
phases.goal: Train scale-specific MC-faithful experts so scale_regret (J_shared - J_expert) is MEASURED not asserted, at the scales where it is affordable, AND gate every reported regret on the stop-condition-#2 check (expert must beat the best heuristic) so a weak 1-seed expert cannot produce garbage NRegret.
phases.run_matrix: (1) EXPERTS via train_mc_faithful at N=336 grid(8,8,3) and N=660 grid(11,11,3), 1 seed x 80 steps x 100 trials, mm_high(0.35,0.25), trained UNDER the fixed_service R_d for that N (R_d(336)=10, R_d(660)=14 verified), save_checkpoint for reuse. N=120 expert = the A1 checkpoints (free). EXPERT MODE SCOPE: experts are trained at the fixed_service R_d, so the expert-based scale_regret/NRegret is reported for the fixed_service mode ONLY; under fixed_protocol (R_d=6) the expert is mis-specified, so fixed_protocol regret-vs-expert is declared UNDEFINED (heuristic-comparison only there). (2) STOP-CONDITION-#2 GUARD: before reporting any NRegret at a scale, assert the expert J beats the best-heuristic J AND |J_heuristic - J_expert| > delta (a denominator-magnitude floor); normalized_scale_regret only nan-guards non-finite costs, NOT a near-zero denominator, so a 1-seed expert that fails to beat heuristics (likely, given the EV1/EV2 env-domination finding) would emit meaningless finite NRegret. If the guard fails, declare NRegret N/A at that scale and attribute low regret to LOW topology-learning headroom, citing the direct_edge_logit_oracle headroom (esp_baselines, already built) as the headroom reference.
phases.model_seeds: Experts: 1 seed each at N=336, N=660 - EXPLICITLY compute-limited (9.1); the expert is a reference oracle for the regret denominator, not the headline policy. NO expert at N>=1248: this is a STRONGER concession than 'absent only at N>=1248' admits - N=1248 IS the spec-6.2 REQUIRED 1000-node scale, so the expert comparison (spec 7.6#3, constraint #6) is satisfied only at N in {120,336,660} and is compute-limited-ABSENT at the required 1000/3000 scales; stated explicitly, not hidden.
phases.trials_per_cell: 100 train trials/step (matches A1/EV5 trainer default). Expert training cost: N=336 ~5.87*2.3 s/step, N=660 ~5.87*7.3 s/step (per-trial ratios from measured eval timing), 80 steps each ~= 76 min total.
phases.constraints_addressed: Scale-specific experts present at the certifiable scales (constraint #6, spec 7.6#3). Full-physics train (link_override=None). Hash-bound checkpoints (save_checkpoint verifies hash on load). Stop-condition #2 guard wired (not just mentioned) so NRegret is never reported off an ill-conditioned denominator. Expert mode-scope made explicit (fixed_service only).
phases.notes: Experts ABSENT at the required 1000/3000 scales is stated as a real spec-6.2/7.6 concession, not softened. EXPERT-QUALITY RISK actioned: a 1-seed REINFORCE expert that only reaches ~96% of distance can be WEAKER than the shared checkpoint -> the direct_edge_logit_oracle headroom is reported alongside at each scale; if the oracle does not beat heuristics at a scale, low regret is attributed to low topology-learning headroom (the env-domination finding), not to a strong shared checkpoint. Runs BEFORE A3b (experts feed the regret denominator); cannot fully overlap A3b (CPU-serial single machine), but CAN overlap A2's eval as a separate background job. The evaluate_macro tail plumbing (T_confirm/latency_cvar/mean_energy) lands in A0; A3a only consumes it.

phases.goal: Eval the N=120-trained shared checkpoints (A1, 5 seeds) across the _GRID_TABLE ladder under BOTH calibration modes vs distance, uniform, and the scale-specific experts. Report UN-GATED performance cost J=1-P_correct (per A0 pre-registration) + F_wrong/F_split UCB (feasible_ucb, reported as a SEPARATE reliability column) + T_confirm/CVaR/energy + scale_regret + NRegret (guarded) + feasibility_retention. EXPOSE fixed-protocol deadline degradation (do NOT hide F_deadline growth as the consensus diameter ~sqrt(N) outruns R_d=6).
phases.run_matrix: Scales N in {120,336,660,1248,3036,9840} via grid_for_target_N (actual N = 120/336/660/1248/3036/9840). Modes: fixed_protocol (constant R_d=6 -> exposes deadline collapse) AND fixed_service_profile (R_d(N)=6/10/14/19/30/54, the PRE-REGISTERED rule). Modes are IDENTICAL at N=120 (factor=1) -> single shared cell there. Policies/cell: shared-ESP (5/3/2 seeds, seed-level reported), scale-expert (fixed_service only, N=336/660), distance, uniform_esp. Performance comparison = UN-GATED J=1-P_correct (A0 pre-registration); F_wrong_UCB/F_split_UCB reported separately. COMPUTE-LIMITED TAPER (N: trials/scene, shared_seeds, modes) - all measured-cost-derived: 120:(200,5,1mode-shared) HEADLINE; 336:(150,5,2modes); 660:(100,3,2modes); 1248:(40,3,2modes); 3036:(16,2,fixed_protocol-ONLY) compute-limited; 9840:(8,2,fixed_protocol-ONLY,shared+distance-only) APPROXIMATION-LABELED. fixed_service is DROPPED at N=3036 and N=9840 (empirically fixed_service ~ fixed_protocol cost at these N because the MC first-hits before R_d epochs, so the extra R_d buys no information for an already-uninformative Wilson CI) -> NO fixed_service point exists at N~3000/10000, stated with reason; this means the spec-6.2 required ladder is met in fixed_protocol at all of {120,336,660,1248,3036} and in fixed_service at {120,336,660,1248} - the required 3000 fixed_service point is a labeled compute-limited drop. scenes=2 every cell (labeled compute-limited vs 30).
phases.model_seeds: Shared ESP: 5 (N=120,336 - full headline), 3 (N=660,1248 - compute-limited 9.1), 2 (N=3036,9840 - compute-limited/approximation-labeled). Experts: 1 (N=336,660, fixed_service only, compute-limited), absent N>=1248. Heuristics deterministic (1). Every reduced-seed cell calls validate_manifest with its actual min_seeds + compute_limited=True (A0).
phases.trials_per_cell: Tapered 200/150/100/40/16/8 per scene x2 scenes. Headline separation/regret from the seed-level nested bootstrap (per-seed P_correct as observations, paired CRN), NOT the pooled binomial. N=9840 16 pooled trials -> Wilson half-width ~+-0.12 (wider than the entire 0.06 uniform-to-distance spread); drawn as a BOUND/annotation in figures, never as a measured data point, and never a validated N=10000 claim (constraint #8).
phases.constraints_addressed: BOTH fixed-protocol AND fixed-service reported (fixed-service up to N=1248); fixed-protocol deadline degradation EXPOSED not hidden (spec 6.5#1). macro_F_wrong/F_split with Wilson UCB via feasible_ucb, reported as a SEPARATE reliability column (#5,#11). Compared vs experts (fixed_service, N<=660) AND heuristics (#6). Pre-registered R_d rule, not result-tuned (#10). N=9840 approximation-labeled, N=3036 fixed_service dropped+labeled (#8, spec 6.2/7.6). >=5 seeds at headline scales 120/336 asserted via min_seeds=5; every reduction labeled compute-limited (9.1).
phases.notes: BINDING COMPUTE CELL (~5h, was over-estimated at 13-30h by both reviews). The reviews assumed (a) 0.4 s/trial @N=120 and (b) fixed_service cost ~ N^1.5 via the R_d multiplier. MEASURED on this machine: 0.14 s/trial @N=120, 0.34 @336, 0.88-1.06 @660, ~4.5-4.8 @1248 - and crucially fixed_service(R_d=19) ~= fixed_protocol(R_d=6) at N=1248 because the MC first-hits the basin/deadline before R_d epochs, so R_d barely multiplies cost. The N^1.5 fixed_service explosion does NOT materialize at the scales run; it would only bite if trials survived to R_d epochs, which they do not. fixed_protocol F_deadline WILL rise sharply at large N - the EXPECTED 13.4 headline finding (protocol/service mismatch recovered by fixed_service), reported not mistaken for GNN model failure. The 3 extra heuristics (link_quality/load_balanced/region_bridge) are OMITTED from the per-scale sweep (distance+uniform only) to fund scale coverage; their N=120 spread is established by EV2 headroom_mc, and the per-scale oracle-headroom requirement at N>120 is a labeled compute-limited drop of G-ESP-BASELINE-ORACLE (closed at N=120 only by EV2). NEXT-CUT if wall-clock tightens: drop N=3036 from the headline (keep 120/336/660/1248 = spec-6.2 required ladder) and carry 3036/9840 as approximation only.

phases.phase: A4 - G-ESP-OOD-GENERALIZATION (declared OOD ablation, one axis at a time)
phases.goal: Eval the N=120 shared checkpoint (5 seeds, trained on mm_high(0.35,0.25)) on OOD regimes it was NOT trained on, EXPLICITLY declared an OOD ablation (in-distribution established first by A2). One distribution axis varied at a time (spec 8.2), holding ALL other axes at the training values.
phases.run_matrix: All @N=120 grid(5,5,3). 3 OOD AXES (4 cells, one a same-axis no-harm level), each with EVERY other axis pinned to the training values to honor one-axis-at-a-time: (1) covariance-family axis: scenario='iid' with base_node_err=0.35 AND corr_strength=0.25 held EXPLICITLY (NOT the build defaults base_err=0.2/corr=0.3 - passing scenario='iid' alone would silently change 3 axes; this is the reviewer-caught A4 bug, fixed); (2) correlation axis: mm_high corr_strength=0.45, base_node_err=0.35 held; (3) base-error axis HARDER: base_node_err=0.50, corr_strength=0.25 held; (4) base-error axis EASIER no-harm: base_node_err=0.20, corr_strength=0.25 held (labeled a same-axis second level, not a 4th distinct axis). Per cell: shared-ESP (5 seeds, seed-level reported) + distance + uniform under the dynamic-MC judge. Report feasibility (F_wrong/F_split UCB), domain-regret vs distance (paired CRN), macro/T_confirm/CVaR/energy deltas vs the in-distribution A2 baseline (reused, no recompute).
phases.model_seeds: 5 (reuses the A1 N=120 checkpoints; no new training).
phases.trials_per_cell: 150 trials x 2 scene seeds = 300/seed -> seed-level across 5 seeds per regime; distance/uniform 300 each, paired. scene_seeds=2 labeled compute-limited (9.1).
phases.constraints_addressed: OOD one axis at a time with all other axes pinned (#8, spec 8.2/8.4#2 - the reviewer-caught 3-axis-at-once bug fixed). In-distribution established first (A2). Failure modes reported, no OOD claim from runtime (8.4#3,#4). >=5 seeds (reuses A1). F_wrong/F_split UCB per regime. Declared an ablation, not a headline superiority claim.
phases.notes: Pure eval, reuses A1 checkpoints (no training). If the N=120 checkpoint degrades below distance on a regime, reported as an OOD failure mode / generalization boundary (spec 8.4#3), informing the section-13 verdict. DROPPED vs the spec-8.2 8-axis ideal: density, road-geometry, PHY-load, sensor-group, mobility, service-profile axes - the 3 chosen (covariance family, correlation, base-error) are the axes build_scale_instance/build_overlapping_scenario expose directly; node-count OOD is A3's job; the rest noted compute-limited-deferred (9.1).

phases.goal: Certify the HARD reliability constraints macro_F_wrong + macro_F_split with reliability UCBs (feasible_ucb): eps=1e-3 needs M>=3800 zero-failure trials/cell (the harness's own feasible_point docstring threshold; spec 9.2 says M>~3000 i.e. 3/M=1e-3 at the boundary, so 3800 gives a STRICT <1e-3). Method = large zero-failure pooling on a SINGLE deployable checkpoint (NOT a multi-seed mixture, NOT importance sampling - the snowball physics has no tractable IS proposal and no IS infra is built; IS noted as deferred future work). Be EXPLICIT where eps=1e-3 is CERTIFIED vs only OBSERVED.
phases.run_matrix: Three tracks, all CONDITIONAL on observed zero-failure (the 3/M bound is void the instant one failure occurs). TRACK A (F_split, mostly free): F_split is identically 0 in this regime (EV5 + verified). For a PER-CHECKPOINT cert (not a heterogeneous 5-seed mixture, which only certifies the mixture not a deployable policy), re-pool ONE chosen shared seed's A3 trials at N=120 (and N=336 if it reaches M>=3800 zero-failure); report 'F_split UCB <= 1e-3 IFF the pooled trials show zero split events (checked post-run), else the Wilson UCB at the observed rate'. TRACK B (F_wrong, honest observed-only): in the HEADLINE mm_high regime F_wrong ~0.035-0.0525 for all policies (verified) -> it STRUCTURALLY CANNOT pass a 1e-3 UCB; report the observed Wilson UCB (distance 0.0789) and state plainly that the headline regime is deliberately reliability-stressed and F_wrong is NOT certifiable at 1e-3 (this is the SAME fact that drove the A0 un-gating - A5 and A3 are explicitly cross-referenced). TRACK A-safe (the one NEW cell, a TRUE F_wrong cert): certify the reliability-SAFE regime iid(base_node_err=0.1) at N=120, M=3800 zero-failure trials, on a SINGLE checkpoint (the A2-best seed, labeled a single-checkpoint diagnostic certification per 9.1, NOT a >=5-seed headline; avoids best-by-P_correct selection bias by fixing the seed choice to seed 0 a priori rather than the post-hoc P_correct max) + distance. EV-history shows F_wrong=F_split=0 there, so a zero-failure M=3800 gives 95% UCB ~3/3800 ~ 7.9e-4 < 1e-3 -> CERTIFIED.
phases.model_seeds: Certification pools TRIALS on a SINGLE deployable checkpoint (a 3/M bound on a 5-seed mixture certifies the mixture, not any one policy). Track A: 1 chosen shared seed (N=120, +N=336 if affordable). Track A-safe: 1 a-priori-fixed seed + distance (labeled single-checkpoint diagnostic, 9.1).
phases.trials_per_cell: Track A: reuse one seed's N=120 pooled trials (must reach M>=3800 zero-failure for a strict cert; if A3's 200x2=400 is short of 3800, add the shortfall - ~3000 extra trials @0.15s ~ 8 min). Track A-safe: M=3800 zero-failure x (1 shared seed + distance) @N=120 iid -> 95% UCB ~7.9e-4 < 1e-3.
phases.constraints_addressed: Reliability UCBs for F_wrong/F_split (#5,#11, spec 6.7). eps=1e-3 via M>=3800 zero-failure (strict, exceeds spec-9.2 boundary M~3000). PER-CHECKPOINT cert, not a mixture pool (reviewer-caught). Method (large-M pooling vs IS) declared, IS deferred. Cells where eps=1e-3 is unaffordable/over-budget labeled approximate/observed, not certified (#8). macrostate_v2.
phases.notes: The split between CERTIFIABLE (F_split per-checkpoint where zero-failure; F_wrong only in the safe regime) and OBSERVABLE-ONLY (F_wrong in the stressed headline regime, and ALL outcomes at N>=660 where 3800 zero-failure trials cost ~1h+/cell) IS the honest finding - never a uniform green cert. A5 Track B is explicitly cross-linked to the A0 feasibility un-gating: the same F_wrong>eps fact that makes the stressed regime non-certifiable is why the A3 performance comparison is un-gated. DROPPED: eps=1e-3 cert at N>=660 and any N=10000 cert -> reported approximation, stated explicitly. If ANY wrong/split failure appears in a pool, the 3/M bound is void -> report the Wilson UCB at the observed rate and state eps is not met.

phases.phase: A6 - G-ESP-SCALE-SYNTHESIS (section-13 verdict; figures read result JSONs ONLY)
phases.goal: Combine A2/A3/A4/A5 result JSONs into the section-13 verdict: decide between 13.1 (true performance-scale SUPERIORITY), 13.2 (parity - stable learned constructor matching strong heuristics, transfers well), 13.3 (loses), or 13.4 (fixed-protocol fails / fixed-service succeeds -> calibration-conditional generalization). Figure scripts read clean JSONs only (assert_no_legacy_metrics + forbidden_keys guard on every figure input); no metric recompute.
phases.run_matrix: No new MC. run_phase_a6_synthesis.py + figure scripts LOAD a2/a3/a4/a5 JSONs and emit: (1) budget curve (A2) rising vs the EV1 flat 0.422 overlay, with the seed-level CI; (2) N vs {P_correct, F_deadline, T_confirm, latency_cvar, energy, scale_regret, guarded-NRegret, feasibility_retention} for fixed_protocol AND fixed_service (fixed_service to N=1248), shared vs expert(N<=660) vs distance vs uniform - N=9840 drawn as a bound annotation; (3) OOD delta table (A4); (4) reliability-UCB table (A5: certified-safe vs observed-stressed). PRE-REGISTERED verdict decision tree (declared BEFORE reading A3 results, A0): claim 13.1 ONLY if shared SEED-LEVEL-separately BEATS distance at >=2 headline scales with low guarded regret; 13.2 if shared matches distance (regret~0, transfers); 13.4 if fixed_protocol feasibility/deadline collapses but fixed_service holds. Given EV5 (~96% of distance, seed-level CIs expected to overlap) the HONEST expected landing is 13.2 parity compounded with 13.4 calibration-conditional transfer - stated plainly, not over-claimed.
phases.constraints_addressed: Figures read JSONs only, no recompute (#13). All results hash-bound to a per-cell manifest carrying physics/profile/evidence/scene/protocol/checkpoint hashes (each run_phase writer emits the full manifest block + passes validate_manifest; checkpoint_hash alone is NOT the result manifest). Reports where superiority holds, where only parity, where protocol-calibration (not model generalization) explains success (13.2/13.4). N=9840 carried as approximation, never validated (#8). Guarded-CDQ2 NOT in the verdict (#14).
phases.notes: Verdict tree pre-registered to prevent manufacturing a 13.1 headline the data does not support. The campaign's defensible value: the MC-faithful trainer closing the EV2 gap (A2 rise-vs-flat) + scale-transfer evidence (A3) + the calibration-conditional feasibility story (A3 fixed-proto-vs-service, 13.4). Must SEPARATE fixed-protocol deadline collapse (protocol mismatch, recovered by fixed-service) from any genuine GNN scale failure (spec 6.5#3). Every headline comparison reports BOTH the seed-level nested-bootstrap CI (the decision interval) and the pooled Wilson (diagnostic only); separation claims must survive the seed-level spread.

top_risks: EXECUTION ORDER (highest-value evidence first): A0 (pre-registration + infra: un-gate the feasibility comparison, wire min_seeds=5 assertion + seed-level nested bootstrap + paired CRN, add the train_esp_reinforce snapshot callback, plumb T_confirm/energy into evaluate_macro, DROP D99/CVaR99) -> A2 (the trainer-rises-vs-flat headline, the campaign's core defensible contribution; reuses A1 seeds but needs the ~73-min snapshot re-train) -> A3a experts (regret denominator, BEFORE A3b, can overlap A2 eval) -> A3b (the scale headline, the ~5h binding cell, fixed_protocol+fixed_service to N=1248, fixed_protocol-only at 3036/9840) -> A4 (OOD ablation, pure eval reusing A1) -> A5 (reliability cert, mostly reuses A3 pools + one safe-regime cert) -> A6 (synthesis, no MC). Critical path is blocked until A1 finishes saving all 5 checkpoints (seed0 done, 1-4 still training) - verify each via load_checkpoint hash-check before any eval phase. Realistic single-machine wall-clock ~8.5-10h of bounded background MC across loop wakeups (measured costs, not the prompt's pessimistic 0.4s/trial), plus the A0 engineering.

SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT RISK TO WATCH = the A0 feasibility-gate pre-registration. The verified dynamic-MC F_wrong is ~0.033-0.0525 for EVERY policy while the profile keeps eps_w=eps_s=1e-3, so the originally-planned feasibility-GATED headline_cost returns +inf for ALL policies and scale_regret / normalized_scale_regret / feasibility_retention all collapse to NaN - the entire A3 scale headline would be undefined. This MUST be fixed before any run by pre-registering (hash-bound) the UN-GATED performance comparison J=1-P_correct with feasibility/F_wrong-UCB reported as a separate reliability column, and it must NOT be retro-tuned after seeing results. If this single fix is skipped, every downstream regret number is mathematically void.

STATISTICAL VALIDITY of every separation/regret claim depends on the A0 seed-level nested bootstrap REPLACING aggregate_seed_macros' pooled-binomial Wilson CI (which treats 5 seeds x trials as one n_total binomial and understates model-seed variance). With EV5's +0.040 trained-vs-distance effect narrower than the seed-level spread, trained-vs-distance will almost certainly NOT separate, and even trained-vs-uniform (+0.043) may not survive the 5-observation paired test - so the verdict honestly defaults to 13.2 parity + 13.4 calibration-conditional, NOT 13.1 superiority. Watch that no figure or claim reads the pooled diagnostic CI as the decision interval.

COMPUTE re-costed from MEASURED per-trial times on this machine (0.14s@120, 0.34s@336, ~1s@660, ~4.8s@1248), correcting BOTH reviews: their ~13-30h A3b estimate assumed 0.4s/trial AND fixed_service~N^1.5 via the R_d multiplier - but measured fixed_service(R_d=19)~=fixed_protocol(R_d=6) at N=1248 because the MC first-hits the basin/deadline before R_d epochs, so R_d barely multiplies cost. Real A3b ~5h, total campaign ~8.5h. The residual large-N risk is N=3036/9840: fixed_service is DROPPED there (uninformative + no R_d cost benefit), N=9840 is fixed_protocol-only at 8 trials, drawn as a +-0.12 bound annotation, NEVER a validated N=10000 claim. If wall-clock tightens, the next honest cut is to drop N=3036 from the headline and keep 120/336/660/1248 as the spec-6.2 required ladder.

EXPECTED-AND-MUST-BE-SURFACED findings (not bugs): (a) fixed_protocol F_deadline rises sharply at large N as the consensus diameter ~sqrt(N) outruns R_d=6 - this is the 13.4 protocol/service-mismatch story recovered by fixed_service, NOT GNN model failure (spec 6.5#3), and the synthesis must separate them. (b) The 1-seed scale experts (compute-limited) may be WEAKER than the shared checkpoint, making NRegret ill-conditioned (near-zero/negative denominator) - the A3a stop-condition-#2 guard (expert must beat best-heuristic AND |J_heur-J_expert|>delta, else NRegret=N/A with direct_edge_logit_oracle headroom cited) must fire before any NRegret is trusted. (c) Experts are absent at the required 1000/3000 scales - a real spec-6.2/7.6 concession, labeled not hidden.
